gap.title: Talented, but invisible online?
gap.intro: You're booked on referrals and reputation. But new clients can't find you, can't tell you're premium, and can't book without sliding into your DMs. That's not a talent problem. It's a brand problem — and brand problems are fixable.
now: A blurry Linktree doing a website's job
New clients who can only reach you by DM
A feed that looks nothing like how good you are
Charging less than the work is worth
after: A booking-ready website that works the front desk for you
A brand that reads premium before you say a word
Short-form content that pulls appointments, not just likes
The look — and the nerve — to raise your rates
First in line when your city searches "near me"

packages.title: Choose your glow-up
packages.intro: Three ways to work with No Empty Chair — a quick polish, a full identity, or a total takeover. Every one opens with the $200 teardown, so the real work starts with your goals already on the table. And every one is built to do a single thing: keep the chair full.
packages.retainerNote: Want the chair full every month, not just at launch? Ask about The Chair-Filler — monthly content, newsletters, and updates from $750/mo.

consults.title: Rather do it yourself?
consults.intro: Book a session, not the whole studio. Deep strategy sessions for salons who want to build it themselves but need the map first. Every one opens with the $200 teardown, so the call isn't spent getting up to speed — it's spent on you. Your fee credits toward a full package if you upgrade within 30 days.

consults.intensiveNote: Want the whole picture in one sitting? The Full Intensive — 90 minutes across social, site & brand, a complete DIY roadmap, and a 2-week follow-up — $500.
consults.addonNote: Add a take-home companion document to any DIY session for $50 — every step written out so you can build from it long after the call. And once you're a regular, follow-up bookings are 10% off after your third session.

alacarte.title: Not ready for the whole thing? Take a piece.
alacarte.intro: Some salons want the full glow-up. Others just need one thing done right. Share your vision and No Empty Chair prices it to fit — no package required. It still opens with the $200 teardown, so even a single piece is built on what your salon actually needs.

work.note: The No Empty Chair method is the difference between a post that 200 people scroll past and one that fills a week of appointments. Your transformation gets hosted right here next.

founder.name: Hi, I'm Makayla.
paragraphs: I'm Makayla Richards. I've spent my career building brands and short-form content at the highest level of media — the same instincts that make videos travel and brands stick. Now I point them at the salon chair.

I take my beauty appointments seriously, and I kept noticing the same thing: the most talented person in the room had the weakest presence online. The braider whose work belongs in a magazine had an overgrown Linktree and a blurry cutout of herself. AI made it easy for anyone to throw something together — which is exactly why so much of it now looks identical. The part that makes a client trust you, the taste and the judgment, is still the part that takes a real eye.

This work is in my blood. My grandmother co-owned a salon, and I come from a line of people who built their own tables when nobody handed them a seat. I also come with a deep bench — photographers, videographers, media and SEO strategists, streaming and digital pros, and the people who actually run this city's events. When your salon needs more than I keep in-house, I already know exactly who to call.

No Empty Chair exists to close that gap, to give the people who are genuinely the best the brand, the site, and the content that finally match their talent, so the right clients find you, trust you, and book you.

steps.title: $200 teardown, first
steps.desc: Every project starts here. A short video breaks down your highest-impact fixes and gathers what your salon needs, so no paid time later is spent getting up to speed. The $200 credits toward whatever comes next.

steps.title: No Empty Chair builds it
steps.desc: Pick your package and the brand, site, and content get built to match your vibe and your goals — with the teardown already pointing the way.

steps.title: You get booked
steps.desc: Launch a brand that looks as good as your work, and watch the right clients start finding their way to your chair.

faq.title: The things salon owners ask me

items.q: What does No Empty Chair actually do?
items.a: No Empty Chair is a salon brand and content studio in Atlanta, built for one kind of person — the stylist, braider, or barber whose work is better than their internet. It builds the brand, the booking-ready website, and the short-form content that get the right clients to find you and book you.

items.q: How much does a salon website and brand cost?
items.a: Packages run from $1,500 for a focused refresh to $5,500+ for a full rebrand with content. Most salons start with the $200 teardown — a short video breaking down your highest-impact fixes — and that $200 credits toward whatever you build next.

A short, honest video breaking down what's quietly costing you bookings — your feed, your site, your booking flow — and the three changes that would move the needle first. It's $200, and it credits toward any package.

items.q: Why does every project start with a teardown?
items.a: Because the worst way to spend a paid session or a build is getting up to speed. The $200 teardown does the information-gathering first — your feed, your site, your goals, your numbers — so by the time the real work starts, No Empty Chair already knows your salon. Nothing after it is spent on catch-up, and the $200 credits toward whatever you do next.

items.q: Do you only work with salons in Atlanta?
items.a: The studio is in Atlanta, and a local chair is always welcome. But websites, branding, and reels travel — if your salon is anywhere and your presence online doesn't match your talent, No Empty Chair can work with you.

items.q: I already have an Instagram. Why would I need more?
items.a: A feed of good photos isn't a brand, and it isn't a booking system. No Empty Chair turns scattered posts into a look that reads premium, a website that takes the appointment, and reels built to be shared — not just scrolled past.

items.q: Is there any ongoing cost after the project's done?
items.a: Yes — a little, and No Empty Chair is upfront about it. A presence that actually books clients runs on real tools, and a few carry a small ongoing cost that stays with you: your domain renews yearly, your site lives on a platform you choose (Wix, Squarespace, WordPress — your call), and any newsletter or scheduling tools set up for you bill on their own. No Empty Chair always points you to the lowest-cost options that don't cheapen the result, custom-styles them so they still look like you, and tells you the real number before anything starts. There's no free work here — free is exactly what kept your brand stuck — just a clear-eyed picture of what a premium brand takes to build, and to keep.
A Refresh is usually a couple of weeks. A full Statement rebrand runs longer, because it includes shooting and building content. You'll have a clear timeline before anything starts.

items.q: Why not just use AI or a template myself?
items.a: AI can get anyone to a generic 80% now — which is exactly why so many salon pages are starting to look the same. The last stretch — taste, cohesion, the details a client feels but can't name — is where trust is won, and it's the whole job. No Empty Chair is run by Makayla Richards, who spent a career engineering short-form video at the highest level of media — 2× Emmy-nominated, Murrow-winning, top 2% across TEGNA — and points that same eye at your chair. Pretty is the floor. Booked is the point.

radar.sub: No Empty Chair is always scouting salons doing incredible work. Drop your details and find out if it already has an eye on you.
radar.successBody: You're officially on the No Empty Chair radar. Keep an eye on your inbox — a reply is coming soon.
